commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=6a8f23c4fcecf0e21767e3e40f393c4c6ad04266)
commands.review_summary:
The PR removes the one-shot gate from macOS status-item visibility-defaults repair and updates focused status-item lifecycle tests to expect repeated cleanup.

Reproducibility: yes. From source inspection on the PR branch, set `didRepairKey = true` and `NSStatusItem VisibleCC codexbar-codex = false`, then call the repair helper; the `codexbar-` filter still matches and removes the key.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the PR has a P1 user-settings compatibility bug and missing external real behavior proof that automation cannot supply for the contributor.

target.title: Repair hidden status item defaults repeatedly
target.body:
## Why?

CodexBar randomly disappearing after updates, external screen disconnections, or when interacting with Bartender, is a major pain. Restarting the app would be a logical recovery method, but it currently doesn't work b/c the repair doesn't happen on repair. This PR fixes it.

## Summary
- remove the one-shot gate from hidden macOS status-item visibility defaults repair
- keep the existing repair marker as historical state, but continue scanning current defaults on each startup
- preserve the CodexBar-owned key filter so unrelated apps' `NSStatusItem VisibleCC ...` keys and true values are left intact

## Related
- #1109
- #1169
- #861

## Tests
- `swift test --filter StatusItemControllerSplitLifecycleTests`
target.author: thinkscape

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
